comment_prompt: What were the strengths of this course and/or this instructor?
comments_json: ["Thoughtfully engaged in an asynchronous course.", "While the course was only online, attending the Thursday evening office hours sessions with the instructor was (usually) interesting and helpful.  However, at times a single student dominated the hour with personal opinions which were unhelpful to learning and led to a lack of enthusiasm to engage  during virtual  office hours.", "Well–organized content and resources, on top of that clear communication and feedback by the instructor.", "Well–organized content and relevant case studies to reinforce key lessons.", "The instructors communication was an area of strength.", "There were office hours that we available.", "It was really interesting to interact with AI systems in a manner that I've never done before. course_info: - TRACE report for CS5047-04 Exploring AI Trends and Tools  (Daniel Feinberg)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["Some of the assignments weren't very clear. Just outlining expectations clearly or providing examples would be good.", "more examples or optional materials that got us into the practical uses of AI. The projects were intensive and excellent, but I would have liked more training on using things like agenic AI in my daily work life to help thoughtfully automate some of my work. I'll be looking for opportunities for this after the program. I do feel like I have a firm foundation to grow from. Recommendations would be helpful for that at the end.", "Instruct the TAs to give uniform guidance vs TAs having to go back to the instructor with questions.  Encourage TAs to respond to student questions directly.  Provide course material with more pro/con views vs a relentless ‘pro–AI’ reading list.  See above about office hours being dominated by students wishing to ‘express an opinion’ vs letting the instructor lead.  The weekly assignments sometimes were not clearly articulated (messy, confusing, contradictory) so it could be hard to know what to submit – leading to guessing.  A more streamlined, specific  assignment outline would be very helpful.", "Could reduce the number of additional resources in some sections of certain modules.", "Less busy work in the assignments", "Much of the weekly modules ended with analysis papers which could have be varied to boost engagement and allow other areas of student's skillset to be shown.", "Today is the last day of the 7.5 week course and I am still awaiting grades and feedback back on assignments submitted from weeks 3 forward. While I respect that the class had many students, perhaps a cap should have been in places so that students may have timely feedback. I am a student that takes professor feedback constructively and incorporates it into my future assignments. I am closing out this course with no pulse on my performance secondary to only 2 of 6 discussion boards being graded and 3 of the 6 weekly assignments (that were 15–20 pages each) graded. Even more discouraging is that I was 2 days late submitting an assignment and was docked 10% from my grade (per the syllabus) but I am not able to hold the professor and TAs to the same standard.", "This certificate is marketed to individuals who are already working and/or have obtained their degree. Most people work at least 40 hours a week, and having hard deadlines limits the ability of working students to be able to get their assignments turned in on time.  Thus, this course would be much better if deadlines did not occur in the middle of the week. Plus, often times, the instructions to the assignments lacked clarity and were confusing. So, it would be much better if students were provided with clear and easy to understand instructions. Lastly, the instructor should provide a grace period for assignments because, again, people are working full time, may have children, & may have other priorities that popup during the week. Thus, when a program is marketed as being self–paced and worker friendly, then it should actually keep its target audience in mind and be more understanding of the target audience."]

course_info: - TRACE report for EEBA6401-01 Business Decision Making  (Julie Meyer)
comment_prompt: Please comment on your experience of the online course environment in the open-ended text box.
comments_json: ["EEBA 6401 used to only be taught by Mark Dockser, and it was famous for being an absolute dumpster fire of a class. I have not met a single person who enjoyed their experience with Mark Dockser teaching, as he was unnecessarily harsh and was not productive or helpful with his feedback. When I saw that Julie Meyer was teaching EEBA 6401 for a change, I upended my entire planned course schedule so that I could take it with her and hopefully avoid the tyranny that was Mark Dockser. Unfortunately, my experience with this class was incredibly bad.This entire class felt like chaotic busywork. I can genuinely say I have never seen an oMBA course run so poorly. The Teaching Team was completely unresponsive for the beginning portion of the class and there was no clarity or communication about what was going on. WHY would you assign teams before the drop deadline?!?! And after a bunch of students drop, why in the world would you continuously scramble the teams and continuously move deadlines and assignments without appropriately communicating what is going on?The coursework didn’t feel very challenging or like we were really learning anything. The assignments like the journal entries really had an air of busywork about them that was infuriating. The wording of the assignments was also very confusing with many, many students getting confused about the fact that there were some journal entry OR case weeks while there was one journal entry AND case week. I could tell the Teaching Team was getting frustrated answering questions about this, but if that many people are asking questions, there’s clearly a systemic problem with how you’re communicating. In addition, the Teaching Team would move deadlines or change assignments without saying anything, and acted as though we were being silly for being on top of our assignments and expressing confusion that things were changing without any notification. The oMBA program is for people who have full time jobs and are taking these courses in a manner convenient to them, we are all planners who look ahead, it’s actually pretty insulting that the Teaching Team acted as though we were being unreasonable in wanting clarity and transparency about our assignments.I think the CEO engagement was meant to be a big deal in this course, but it really depended on who you got. My friend had a CEO who gave a really thoughtful feedback, but I’m pretty sure mine just stuck our assignment into ChatGPT and then spat some analysis back at us.I don’t feel like I actually learned anything in this course. It’s truly unfortunate this is a required course because I would never recommend it to anyone."]

course_info: - TRACE report for EEBA6401-01 Business Decision Making  (Julie Meyer)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["Julie Meyer was a HORRIBLE communicator at the beginning of the course in that she didn’t communicate at all. The way she handled making the teams and the changing assignment schedule was absolutely ridiculous. The oMBA program is specifically for people who have full time jobs and is marketed as being incredibly flexible and understanding of this fact. However, Julie Meyer kept changing up groups and assignment schedules as though we would be grateful for the changing deadlines. Her comment that “We were not aware most of you were as far as you were” about many groups already working on the assignment suggested to me that she doesn’t actually understand who her students are and that we are all planning around busy life and work schedules to complete our degrees.Her flippant comments such as “Given this is an MBA course, hopefully this will be a bit of an example of resilience, pivoting and course–correcting when things don’t go as planned (which they often don’t in business)!” suggests she REALLY can’t read a room. I’m sure she thought she was being funny and turning the Teaching Team’s shortcomings into a teaching moment for us, but in reality all she did was frustrate the students who felt like she wasn’t taking our concerns seriously.I will be avoiding any future classes that she teaches.", "Sort out a reasonable timeline for the class to avoid shifting deadlines."]

course_info: - TRACE report for MSCI6003-01 Healthcare Leadership Seminar  (Elyse Watkins)
comment_prompt: Please comment on your experience of the online course environment in the open-ended text box.
comments_json: ["While this is not my first online 7 week course as part of the DMSc graduate program, it is the first course in which I had to complete a separate course (AMA tutorial) in order to properly complete the assignments. Expecting students to be able to complete a separate multi–assessment intensive tutorial as part of this course without accounting for the time to take it within the first week of classes when assignments are due was challenging.", "There was no interaction with classmates. I enjoy opportunities to learn from classmates experiences and that was not available in this class.", "Returning to school after thirteen years has been both exciting and challenging. Adjusting to an online doctorate program took some effort since it’s very different from the traditional classroom setting I was used to. Still, the experience has been great overall. The resources are easy to access, and the support has been strong. I put in a lot of time to stay on top of things, but it’s worth it. I’m learning and growing in ways that remind me why I came back to school in the first place.", "This class utilized a new system for me, AMA.  Since I was new to this,  my instructor provided me with support and guidance to correct the challenge and get me the information I required to become successful in the course.", "I enjoyed the course very much.", "Very flexible timing and asynchronous"]

course_info: - TRACE report for MSCI6003-01 Healthcare Leadership Seminar  (Elyse Watkins)
comment_prompt: What were the strengths of this course and/or this instructor?
comments_json: ["The strengths were that the this course/instructor(s) were available to answer questions via scheduled office hours/email and assignments were graded with a relatively quick turnaround to ascertain instructor feedback and areas of improvement.", "These questions don't seem relevant to a class that is asynchronous and 100% online. There is no in–person interaction unless you schedule an appointment.  Dr. Watkins always responded to emails in a timely fashion and was helpful.", "1. One of the greatest strengths of this course was Dr. Douglas Bruce’s lectures. Even though they were prerecorded, they felt personal and engaging, almost like he was teaching live. He’s easy to listen to and never dull. His enthusiasm and expertise shine through, and the stories and photos he shares/incorporates from his career and travels make learning both enjoyable and memorable. My biggest takeaway was his emphasis on “behavior change” and the importance of asking “why” until you reach the root cause.2. Dr. Watkins has also been an outstanding instructor. Her feedback was detailed, timely, and always constructive. This was my first class using AMA format, and her guidance helped me smoothly transition from the APA style I was familiar with. I’m grateful for her support, patience, and encouragement throughout the course. Her thoughtful feedback made a real impact on my learning and growth as both a student and a professional.", "This course was well designed and I learned a significant amount regarding Healthcare Leadership.  I also learned how to use AMA instead of APA style.", "She responded in a timely fashion and was available to offer feedback."]

course_info: - TRACE report for MSCI6003-01 Healthcare Leadership Seminar  (Elyse Watkins)
comment_prompt: What could the instructor do to make this course better?
comments_json: ["–Include class discussion posts to facilitate a more inclusive learning environment in which individuals are actually utilizing the leadership skills in a real life situation rather than simply writing about it through weekly assignments. It seems counter–intuitive to write about leadership skills that we're learning/trying to implement with no context/awareness to how peers are responding to the information provided and working in a silo based on examples from our own experiences.–While the information from the lecture was helpful in understanding the root of leadership styles and navigating corporate politics in order to grow within our role, the course seemed rushed and the assignments didn't feel adequately reflective of the course overview/outcomes goals. Due to the various natures of students' careers,  it would have been helpful to have more of a hands on approach to the course content as opposed to assignments that were solely focused on writing skills.–Sometimes, the information that is provided in the weekly assignment tab differs from other resources/tabs available such as page count on the syllabus being 1–2 pages for an assignment, but the module will state 3–4 pages. It becomes difficult to determine that the correct information is, and due to the fact that several weeks had 2 assignments due at a time, this lack of clarity posed a challenge.–Provide more feedback related to the content of the submitted assignments and not solely on the grammar/editorial aspects. Nearly every assignment, I didn't feel that I was gaining anything meaningful from the instructor feedback other than the technical elements, once again reinforcing the idea that this felt more akin to a language composition course than a healthcare leadership course.", "Listening to lectures followed by online videos/TED talks is too much screen time for me and not my preferred method for learning. I would have preferred that the class have more reading and less video material. A transcript of Dr. Bruce's lectures would also be helpful.", "The course was very well organized and easy to follow. We didn’t have traditional discussion posts, but we did have a discussion board where we could post questions. That was helpful for getting clarification, though adding a few structured discussion opportunities might help students connect more and share perspectives. Overall, the instructor created a positive and supportive learning environment.", "Nothing.", "Nothing, she did excellent."]

course_info: - TRACE report for MSCI6003-01 Healthcare Leadership Seminar  (Elyse Watkins)
comment_prompt: Please expand on the instructor’s strengths and/or areas for improvement in facilitating inclusive learning.
comments_json: ["–Offer the slides of the recorded lecture as a standard PDF file. The PDF of the presentation was only available as a 'notes' version, in which the slide thumbnails were extremely small, making the text difficult to read and not really an effective use of a supplementary resource to aid listening to the recorded lecture–Provide transcripts with the recorded lectures + include pertinent information on the slides. For this lecture, the slides focused on quotes or images that the instructor briefly touched on, but the core of the information was not displayed on the slides, making the information difficult to digest for those that are not strong auditory learners. It was also challenging when the small icon of the instructor blocks a segment of the text/quote on the slide but there is no transcript to compare it to, and the PDF does not have the text legible.–Include a template/example of what a 'great' assignment is to set expectations for the assignments", "I can’t think of any major areas for improvement, their approach already models the kind of inclusive, student–centered leadership we study in this program.", "This instuctor is very knowledgeable in Healthcare Leadership and Medical Science.  She is very motivating, we spoke about future classes and research topics.  Very helpful!", "N/a"]

comments_json: ["There was no online experience that created a sense of community. No discussion sections and reading materials are posted in a manner that wasn't helpful compared to other classes.
